steps.title: Use random selection
steps.content: Random selection determines which population the results can be applied to. Random assignment helps support a cause-and-effect conclusion about the watering patterns, but it does not expand the population to a new age group or a new location.

steps.title: Identify the source population
steps.content: The $60$ trees were randomly selected from the $14$-year-old mangrove trees in this habitat. Therefore the results can be generalized to that source population. The answer cannot be only the selected sample, because random selection allows generalization beyond those $60$ trees.

steps.title: Keep the age and location
steps.content: The sample did not come from all mangrove trees in Belize, and it did not come from $17$-year-old trees. The largest supported population is all mangrove trees that were $14$ years old in this habitat. The answer is D.
